title: Workstate snapshot (v5)
description: The single versioned artifact Workstate compiles and RexOps consumes as its source of truth. Envelope { schema_version, built_at } wrapping four Section<T> objects (scripts, tools, findings, jobs). Modeled from workstate/src/model/snapshot.rs plus the normalized inventory types, cross-checked against the live RexOps fixture. Workstate is strictly read-only and never mutates source tools.
$comment: SEAMS (intentional, documented): (1) tools[].review_due remains date-or-null but currently stays null for RexOps compatibility; ToolFoundry's concrete lifecycle review date is the additive tools[].review_after field and the live due signal is review_due_flag. (2) tools.tool_count / attention_count are producer-reported summaries, advisory only, not cross-checked against tools[].length. (3) Record objects allow additionalProperties because producers preserve unmodeled upstream keys: Script flattens them inline (serde flatten). Finding does NOT: as of v4 it carries an explicit field allowlist with no passthrough 'rest' bag, so an unmodeled Bulwark wire field (which can hold matched secrets/PII) never reaches the snapshot. (4) section.data is null when a section is Missing/Failed/UnsupportedVersion — graceful degradation, not an error. (5) v4 adds provenance.dropped_records (count of records normalization dropped for want of a usable id/subject) and a FreshnessUnknown status (a clean read whose source age is unknown, not mislabeled Fresh). Obsolete v1 fields (source_tool, projects[], top-level generated_at) are intentionally absent; additionalProperties:true at the root means a stray legacy key degrades rather than hard-failing, matching consumer leniency.

schema_version.description: Major contract version. RexOps gates on this FIRST; only v5 is consumed.

built_at.description: When this snapshot was compiled (RFC3339 UTC). Replaces the obsolete v1 'generated_at'.

fetched_at.description: When Workstate READ the feed (UTC); null if never fetched (Missing).

source_observed_at.description: When the SOURCE tool generated the data (UTC); null if unknown. The real staleness signal.

dropped_records.description: v4: how many raw records this section's normalization dropped for want of a usable id/subject. 0 for a clean compile and for Missing/Failed/UnsupportedVersion sections. Makes per-record loss visible instead of silent.

status.description: Workstate's freshness verdict. A known string (Fresh | Stale | FreshnessUnknown | Missing — FreshnessUnknown added in v4 for a clean read whose source age is unknown), any other string (forward-compat: degrades to Unknown health on the consumer), or a tagged object for the data-bearing variants (UnsupportedVersion, MissingVersion, SourceMismatch, Failed). MissingVersion and SourceMismatch were added in v4. Intentionally NOT a closed enum.

oneOf.$comment: Known bare-string statuses: Fresh, Stale, FreshnessUnknown, Missing. Open by design — an unrecognized value degrades on the consumer rather than failing validation.
Feed declared a schema_version we don't support. found is the declared version (always present for this variant as of v4 — a feed declaring NO version is MissingVersion instead).
v4: feed declared NO schema_version at all — rejected as strictly as a wrong one, but kept distinct so 'forgot to stamp a version' is tellable from 'stamped an unsupported one'.

v4: feed's self-reported source_tool disagreed with the adapter that read it (e.g. a ScriptVault export at the Bulwark path). Data rejected so mislabeled records never enter the snapshot. An empty source_tool is tolerated and does not produce this status.

data.description: ScriptInventory, or null when the section is Missing/Failed/UnsupportedVersion.

items.$comment: additionalProperties:true — Script flattens unmodeled ScriptVault keys inline (serde flatten).

tool_count.description: Advisory: producer-reported, not cross-checked against tools[].length.

review_due.description: Reserved date-or-null field; currently emitted as null for RexOps compatibility.

review_due_flag.description: The live 'review is due' signal from ToolFoundry.

dropped_records.description: v4: findings dropped during normalization (no usable subject). Mirrors provenance.dropped_records.

items.$comment: v4: additionalProperties:false — Finding is an EXPLICIT allowlist with no passthrough 'rest' bag (removed in v4). Unmodeled Bulwark wire fields can hold matched secrets/PII, so they are dropped at normalization and rejected here rather than baked into the persisted snapshot. The audited non-sensitive fields consumers need (path/risk/owner) are promoted to first-class allowlisted fields below.

severity.description: Normalized bucket info|low|medium|high|critical|unrated|unknown. String, not enum, so an unforeseen value degrades not fails.

location.description: Always present ('unknown' when the source gave none).

path.description: v4: filesystem path of the scanned script (Bulwark `path`), if present. Omitted when absent. Downstream consumers (toolbox-bridge) key sidecars by this.

risk.description: v4: Bulwark's free-form risk label, if present. Omitted when absent.

owner.description: v4: owning user/team (Bulwark `owner`), if present. Omitted when absent.

dropped_records.description: v5: runs dropped during normalization (no usable id). Mirrors provenance.dropped_records.

items.$comment: v5: Job is an EXPLICIT allowlist (deny_unknown_fields) — id, title, outcome only. Proto's raw step counts/timestamps are NOT persisted; the adapter derives outcome from status + failed.

title.description: Protocol title; 'protocol run' when the feed gave none.

outcome.description: Derived: failed>0 -> failed; complete & no failures -> passed; else running.
